nodes.title: Capture baseline metrics and add acceptance harness
nodes.description:
Add regression coverage for the Allium indexing/runtime behaviour contract before major implementation lanes begin. This is the first executable task — it turns the Allium contract into guardrails before changing runtime architecture.

## Scope

- Create tests that assert cold-start readiness behaviour (queries during indexing return deferred/not-ready, not empty success).
- Create tests that assert deterministic indexing output (same input → same output across runs).
- Create tests that assert watcher/indexer eligibility parity (same files supported by both).
- Create tests that assert writer chunk boundedness (chunks never exceed configured writer_chunk_size).
- Create tests that assert shared DB lifecycle metadata is queryable (stats, root counts, maintenance timestamps).
- Create tests that assert hot-file reparse fallback correctness (cache miss → cold parse, never stale-tree bug).

## Allium sections covered

- QueryDefersUntilIndexIsUsable
- QueryServesFromReadyOrPartialIndex
- InitialIndexQueuesOnlySupportedFiles
- WatcherUsesTheSameEligibilityPolicyAsTheIndexer
- WriterCreatesBoundedChunks
- ReadyTasksJoinChunksUntilSizeLimit
- HotFilesPreferIncrementalReparse
- IncrementalChangeFallsBackToColdParse
- ProjectReturnsToReadyWhenPendingWorkDrains
- SharedProjectBecomesStaleWhenMissingOnDisk
- SharedProjectBecomesStaleWhenInactive
- StaleProjectsArePrunedOnlyWhenIdle

## Invariants to test

- QueriesAreOnlyServedFromUsableIndexes
- UnsupportedFilesNeverProduceParseTasks
- IncrementalTasksAreAlwaysClassified
- ChunksRemainBounded
- PrunedProjectsHaveNoPendingWork
nodes.acceptance:
- Cold-start readiness behaviour is tested: queries during cold_start/indexing/maintenance return deferred status with retry_after.
- Deterministic indexing output is tested: same repo indexed twice produces identical results.
- Watcher/indexer eligibility parity is tested: supported-file rules produce same results in both paths.
- Writer chunk boundedness is tested: no chunk exceeds config.writer_chunk_size (50).
- Shared DB lifecycle metadata is testable: stats endpoint returns DB size, root counts, maintenance timestamps.
- Hot-file reparse fallback is tested: cache miss triggers cold parse, never produces incorrect results.
- All Allium invariants have at least one test exercising them.
nodes.context: Primary docs: .allium/qartez-indexing-improvements.allium (all rules and invariants). Implementation playbook: docs/agents/high-roi-implementation-playbook.md (lane descriptions). This task must land BEFORE any architecture changes to provide a safety net.

nodes.title: Add explicit readiness signalling and partial-result contract
nodes.description:
Implement explicit readiness states so that tool queries during cold start, indexing, or maintenance receive a structured deferred response instead of silently returning empty results.

## Behavioural claims

- When a project is in cold_start, indexing, or maintenance readiness, queries are deferred with a retry_after duration.
- When a project is in ready or partial_reindex and the reader pool is ready, queries are served.
- Partial completeness is reported when serving from a partial_reindex state.
- The DatabaseCatalog transitions from opening→ready before queries are served.

## Implementation areas

- DatabaseCatalog state machine: opening → ready
- ProjectIndex readiness states: cold_start → indexing → ready / partial_reindex / maintenance / failed
- QuerySession lifecycle: waiting_for_readiness → deferred | served | rejected
- MCP tool response format: include readiness status and retry_after when deferred
nodes.acceptance:
- Cold-start query before index completion returns explicit deferred/not-ready status with retry_after duration, not empty success.
- Tool reads can proceed while writer activity is active when a usable index exists (ready or partial_reindex state).
- Partial completeness is reported in query responses during partial_reindex.
- DatabaseCatalog reader_pool transitions from warming to ready before any query is served.
- All readiness state transitions are covered by tests from the baseline harness.
nodes.context: Allium rules: DatabaseCatalogOpens, ReaderPoolBecomesReadyBeforeQueriesAreServed, ToolQueryCreatesSession, QueryDefersUntilIndexIsUsable, QueryServesFromReadyOrPartialIndex. Config: readiness_retry_after = 2.seconds.

nodes.title: Split SQLite access into dedicated writer path plus read pool
nodes.description:
Separate the SQLite access path so that indexing, watcher writes, prune, and compact operations use one controlled writer path, while tool handlers use a read pool. This prevents heavy write activity from starving read requests.

## Goals

- One writer path for all mutation operations (indexing, watcher, prune, compact).
- Read pool for tool handler queries.
- Writer state is visible: idle | full_indexing | incremental_indexing | pruning | compacting | blocked.
- Read pool state is visible: warming | ready | degraded.
- No silent empty results during write-heavy periods.

## Anti-goals

- Do not add mandatory external database services.
- Do not hide transient not-ready conditions behind empty results.
nodes.acceptance:
- Indexing, watcher, prune, and compact all use one controlled writer path.
- Tool reads can proceed concurrently when writer is active and a usable index exists.
- Writer state is queryable and visible to callers (idle, full_indexing, incremental_indexing, pruning, compacting, blocked).
- Read pool state is queryable (warming, ready, degraded).
- Heavy writer activity does not starve read requests — read latency remains bounded.
- Tests prove read/write isolation under concurrent load.
nodes.context: Allium entity: DatabaseCatalog with writer_state and reader_pool fields. Implementation playbook Lane 1. Hotspots: startup/index orchestration, server DB wiring, watcher/index writer path, tool-handler read path.

nodes.title: Unify watcher eligibility with indexer logic and chunk writer commits
nodes.description:
Make watcher and indexer agree on which files are supported, and split heavy watcher write batches into bounded chunks that are fair to readers.

## Behavioural claims

- Watcher uses the same SupportedSourcePath.matches eligibility check as the indexer.
- Deleted files are always eligible (for cleanup).
- Watch batches move through states: queued → preparing → parsing → writing → completed | failed.
- Write chunks are bounded by config.writer_chunk_size (50).
- Ready parse tasks join chunks until the size limit is reached.
- Each chunk is committed separately, yielding to readers between commits.

## Implementation areas

- src/watch.rs: watcher event batching and eligibility filtering
- Shared supported-file logic between watcher and indexer
- WriteChunk creation and task assignment
- Chunk commit loop with reader yield points
nodes.acceptance:
- Watcher and indexer agree on supported files — same eligibility rules produce identical results.
- Special filenames (spaces, unicode, long paths) stay fresh under watcher.
- Write chunks remain at or below config.writer_chunk_size (50).
- Branch switches or large watcher bursts do not monopolize the DB for long stretches.
- Read latency remains bounded during large watcher bursts.
- WatchBatch state transitions are correct: queued → preparing → parsing → writing → completed/failed.
nodes.context: Allium rules: WatcherUsesTheSameEligibilityPolicyAsTheIndexer, SupportedWatchItemsCreateIncrementalParseTasks, WriterCreatesBoundedChunks, ReadyTasksJoinChunksUntilSizeLimit, WriterCommitsEachChunkSeparately, CommittedChunkYieldsToReaders. Config: writer_chunk_size = 50, large_watch_batch_threshold = 200.

nodes.title: Replace mutexed parser with thread-local, lazy-loaded parser workers
nodes.description:
Remove the single Mutex<Parser> bottleneck by creating thread-local, lazy-loaded parser workers per language. Rust-only repos should not pay startup cost for absent languages.

## Behavioural claims

- ParseWorker is created per project_index with a thread_key and language.
- Parser state is loaded lazily by language on first use.
- Workers transition: idle → loading_language → parsing → idle (or evicted after TTL).
- Idle workers may be evicted after config.parser_idle_ttl (15 minutes).
- ParseTask claims a worker for its source file's language.

## Anti-goals

- Do not eagerly initialize all supported grammars.
- Do not introduce non-deterministic parser sharing bugs.
nodes.acceptance:
- Parser state is loaded lazily by language — no parser is created until its first parse task.
- Rust-only repos do not pay meaningful startup cost for absent languages (Python, Go, etc.).
- Parallel parse workers do not deadlock or serialize on one global parser lock.
- Parser metrics or tests prove per-language lazy activation.
- Idle workers are evicted after config.parser_idle_ttl (15 minutes) of inactivity.
- Worker state transitions are correct: idle → loading_language → parsing → idle → evicted.
nodes.context: Allium rules: ParseTaskClaimsThreadLocalWorkerForLanguage, WorkerLoadsLanguageOnDemand, IdleWorkerMayBeEvicted. Config: parser_idle_ttl = 15 minutes. Hotspots: src/index/parser.rs, parser call sites in src/index/mod.rs.

nodes.title: Parallelize full-index parse and extract while keeping DB writes serialized
nodes.description:
Parallelize the full-index parse/extract phase using the new thread-local parser workers, while keeping DB writes serialized or batched deterministically through the chunk writer.

## Goals

- File parse and extract operations run in parallel across available workers.
- DB writes remain serialized through WriteChunk commits.
- Output ordering is deterministic where downstream behaviour depends on it.
- No direct SQLite writes from individual workers.

## Anti-goals

- Do not write to SQLite directly from every worker.
- Do not change output ordering in a way that destabilizes deterministic downstream behaviour.
nodes.acceptance:
- Full-index wall-clock time drops materially on representative repos (measured against baseline).
- Result sets remain stable across repeated runs — no nondeterministic output drift.
- DB writes are not performed directly from workers — all go through WriteChunk.
- Output ordering remains deterministic for downstream consumers.
- Parse tasks transition correctly: queued → parsing → ready_to_write → written.
nodes.context: Allium rules: InitialIndexQueuesOnlySupportedFiles, ParsedTasksBecomeWriteReadyAndRefreshHotTree, WriterCreatesBoundedChunks, ReadyTasksJoinChunksUntilSizeLimit, WriterCommitsEachChunkSeparately. Hotspot: src/index/mod.rs.

nodes.title: Add hot-file incremental reparsing using cached Tree state
nodes.description:
Reuse recent syntax Tree state for watcher-driven edits to achieve faster reindexing on hot files, with safe fallback to cold parse when the cached tree is unavailable or invalid.

## Behavioural claims

- SourceFile tracks tree_cache state: absent | hot | invalidated | evicted.
- Hot tree is retained for config.hot_tree_retention (30 minutes) after last parse.
- Incremental changes with a hot tree and valid byte-edit info use incremental parse mode.
- Incremental changes without a hot tree or without valid byte-edit info fall back to cold parse.
- Never produce stale-tree correctness bugs.

## Anti-goals

- Do not try to persist syntax trees for the entire repo.
- Do not risk stale-tree correctness bugs for marginal speedups.
nodes.acceptance:
- Repeated edits on hot files reindex faster than cold parse baseline (measured).
- Cache misses fall back to cold parse safely — results are always correct.
- Invalid or unavailable byte-edit information never causes stale-tree correctness bugs.
- Syntax trees are not persisted for the whole repo — only hot files retain trees.
- SourceFile tree_cache transitions are correct: absent → hot → invalidated → evicted.
nodes.context: Allium rules: HotFilesPreferIncrementalReparse, IncrementalChangeFallsBackToColdParse, ParsedTasksBecomeWriteReadyAndRefreshHotTree. Config: hot_tree_retention = 30 minutes. Hotspots: parser/edit path, watcher-driven reindex path.

nodes.title: Add shared-database lifecycle management, stale-root pruning, and maintenance stats
nodes.description:
Make shared --db-path usage visible and manageable: track root lifecycle metadata, support prune/stats/compact operations, and keep normal startup cheap.

## Behavioural claims

- DatabaseCatalog.mode can be dedicated or shared.
- Shared projects become stale when missing on disk or inactive for config.stale_project_window (30 days).
- Prune runs only during maintenance state and only prunes stale projects with no pending work.
- Compact runs only when DatabasePolicy.compaction_due returns true.
- Catalog returns to ready after maintenance completes.
- Stats surface DB size, root counts, and maintenance timestamps.

## Anti-goals

- Do not vacuum/compact unexpectedly on normal startup.
- Do not prune active roots or live data silently.
nodes.acceptance:
- Shared --db-path usage is visible to users via stats command.
- Root lifecycle metadata is tracked (active, stale, pruned).
- Stale roots can be pruned safely — only when they have no pending work.
- Stats surface DB size, root counts, and maintenance timestamps.
- Normal startup does not unexpectedly vacuum, compact, or prune.
- Prune grace period (7 days) is respected before removing stale project data.
- Compact runs only when policy says due, not on every startup.
nodes.context: Allium rules: SharedProjectBecomesStaleWhenMissingOnDisk, SharedProjectBecomesStaleWhenInactive, PruneRunsOnlyDuringMaintenance, StaleProjectsArePrunedOnlyWhenIdle, OptimizeMayRunWhileCatalogIsReady, CompactRunsOnlyWhenPolicySaysDue, CatalogReturnsToReadyAfterMaintenance. Config: stale_project_window = 30 days, prune_grace_period = 7 days.

nodes.title: Finalize regression suite, benchmark report, and operator documentation
nodes.description:
Update rollout documentation, regression coverage, and handoff guidance after all implementation lanes have landed.

## Scope

- Ensure docs/agents guidance reflects implemented behaviour.
- Update Allium spec for any observable behaviour changes not already covered.
- Run full validation: cargo fmt --all -- --check, cargo clippy --all-targets --all-features -- -D warnings, cargo build --release, cargo test --release --no-fail-fast.
- Run installer/bootstrap tests if those scripts were touched.
- Produce benchmark report comparing before/after indexing performance.
nodes.acceptance:
- docs/agents guidance reflects implemented behaviour across all lanes.
- Allium is updated for any observable behaviour changes.
- Full validation passes: fmt, clippy, release build, release tests.
- Installer/bootstrap changes run the relevant install tests.
- Benchmark report documents performance improvements for each lane.
- All discovered follow-up work is filed in Beads.
nodes.context: Use docs/agents/validation-and-handoff.md as the closeout checklist. Allium contract: .allium/qartez-indexing-improvements.allium.

nodes.title: Bootstrap fresh shared-server database should not fail on empty Dolt commit
nodes.description:
Fix the fresh shared-server bootstrap path that fails when Dolt has nothing to commit during initial database creation.

## Reproduction

On Windows PowerShell:
1. `bd bootstrap --dry-run` — plans to create fresh database
2. `bd bootstrap` — fails with 'dolt commit: Error 1105: nothing to commit'
3. `bd init --force` — succeeds but does not set up shared-server mode

## Root cause hypothesis

The bootstrap flow attempts a `dolt commit` immediately after creating an empty database, but Dolt rejects the commit when there are no staged changes.

## Fix approach

- Detect empty initial state before attempting dolt commit.
- Either skip the commit when there is no diff, or stage a minimal sentinel file to ensure there is always something to commit.
- Add a regression test covering the empty fresh DB bootstrap path.
nodes.acceptance:
- Fresh shared-server bootstrap creates the project database successfully.
- `bd ready` succeeds after bootstrap.
- Bootstrap treats an empty initial Dolt commit as success or avoids committing when there is no diff.
- Regression test covers the empty fresh DB bootstrap path.
- Works on both Windows PowerShell and POSIX environments.
nodes.context: Observed on Windows PowerShell with BEADS_DOLT_SHARED_SERVER=1 and BEADS_DOLT_SERVER_MODE=1. This is a discovered-from bug — it does not block the main roadmap but should be fixed to ensure reliable agent setup.
